description: CSS styling language completion data for web design

keywords.description: color - Sets the text color of an element. Accepts various formats: named colors, hex codes, RGB, HSL, and more.
keywords.example: color: #ff0000; /* red */ color: rgb(255, 0, 0); color: blue;

keywords.description: background-color - Sets the background color of an element. Part of the background shorthand property.
margin - Sets the outer spacing around an element. Can be set for all sides or individually (top, right, bottom, left).
padding - Sets the inner spacing within an element between the content and border. Follows same syntax as margin.
border - Shorthand property for setting border width, style, and color. Can be applied to all sides or individually.
keywords.example: border: 1px solid black; border-top: 2px dashed red;

keywords.description: width - Sets the width of an element. Can use pixels, percentages, viewport units, or other CSS units.
height - Sets the height of an element. Similar units to width, but also supports min-height and max-height.
display - Controls how an element is displayed and its layout behavior. Key property for modern CSS layouts.
keywords.example: display: block; display: inline; display: flex; display: grid; display: none;

keywords.description: position - Sets the positioning method for an element. Works with top, right, bottom, left properties.
keywords.example: position: relative; position: absolute; position: fixed; position: sticky;

keywords.description: flex - Shorthand for flex-grow, flex-shrink, and flex-basis. Used in flexbox layouts for flexible sizing.
grid - Shorthand for grid-template properties. Defines grid template areas, rows, and columns.
font-size - Sets the size of the font. Can use absolute units (px, pt) or relative units (em, rem, %).
font-family - Specifies font family for text. Provide fallbacks for better cross-platform compatibility.
keywords.example: font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-family: 'Times New Roman', serif;

keywords.description: text-align - Sets horizontal alignment of text content within its container.
keywords.example: text-align: left; text-align: center; text-align: right; text-align: justify;

keywords.description: opacity - Sets the transparency level of an element. Value between 0 (fully transparent) and 1 (fully opaque).
z-index - Controls the vertical stacking order of elements. Higher values appear in front.
top - Sets the top edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
right - Sets the right edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
bottom - Sets the bottom edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
left - Sets the left edge position of a positioned element relative to its containing block.
float - Places an element on the left or right side of its container, allowing text to wrap around it.
keywords.example: float: left; float: right; float: none;

keywords.description: clear - Specifies which sides of an element where floating elements are not allowed.
keywords.example: clear: both; clear: left; clear: right; clear: none;

keywords.description: overflow - Controls what happens when content overflows an element's box.
keywords.example: overflow: hidden; overflow: scroll; overflow: auto; overflow: visible;

keywords.description: overflow-x - Controls horizontal overflow behavior specifically.
keywords.example: overflow-x: hidden; overflow-x: scroll; overflow-x: auto;

keywords.description: overflow-y - Controls vertical overflow behavior specifically.
keywords.example: overflow-y: hidden; overflow-y: scroll; overflow-y: auto;

keywords.description: visibility - Controls whether an element is visible or hidden while maintaining its layout space.
keywords.example: visibility: visible; visibility: hidden; visibility: collapse;

keywords.description: clip-path - Creates a clipping region to show only part of an element.
transform - Applies 2D or 3D transformations to elements like rotation, scaling, translation.
keywords.example: transform: rotate(45deg); transform: scale(1.2); transform: translate(10px, 20px);

keywords.description: transform-origin - Sets the origin point for transformations.
keywords.example: transform-origin: center; transform-origin: top left; transform-origin: 50% 50%;

keywords.description: transition - Shorthand for transition properties to create smooth animations between property changes.
keywords.example: transition: all 0.3s ease; transition: opacity 1s linear;

keywords.description: transition-property - Specifies which CSS properties should transition.
transition-duration - Sets the duration of transition effects.
transition-timing-function - Sets the speed curve of transition effects.
transition-delay - Sets when the transition effect starts.
animation - Shorthand for animation properties to create keyframe animations.
keywords.example: animation: slideIn 1s ease-in-out; animation: spin 2s linear infinite;

keywords.description: animation-name - Specifies the name of the @keyframes rule to use.
animation-duration - Sets how long an animation takes to complete.
animation-timing-function - Sets the speed curve of the animation.
animation-delay - Sets when the animation starts.
animation-iteration-count - Sets how many times an animation repeats.
animation-direction - Sets whether an animation plays forward, backward, or alternates.
keywords.example: animation-direction: normal; animation-direction: reverse; animation-direction: alternate;

keywords.description: animation-fill-mode - Sets what styles are applied before/after the animation executes.
keywords.example: animation-fill-mode: forwards; animation-fill-mode: backwards; animation-fill-mode: both;

keywords.description: animation-play-state - Sets whether an animation is running or paused.
font-weight - Sets the weight (boldness) of the font.
keywords.example: font-weight: normal; font-weight: bold; font-weight: 400; font-weight: 700;

keywords.description: font-style - Sets the style of the font (normal, italic, oblique).
keywords.example: font-style: normal; font-style: italic; font-style: oblique;

keywords.description: font-variant - Sets whether to display text in small caps or normal.
line-height - Sets the height of lines of text, controlling vertical spacing.
letter-spacing - Sets the spacing between characters in text.
word-spacing - Sets the spacing between words in text.
text-decoration - Sets decorations on text like underline, overline, line-through.
keywords.example: text-decoration: none; text-decoration: underline; text-decoration: line-through;

keywords.description: text-transform - Controls capitalization of text.
keywords.example: text-transform: uppercase; text-transform: lowercase; text-transform: capitalize;

keywords.description: text-indent - Sets the indentation of the first line of text in a block.
text-shadow - Adds shadow effects to text.
white-space - Controls how white space inside an element is handled.
keywords.example: white-space: normal; white-space: nowrap; white-space: pre; white-space: pre-wrap;

keywords.description: word-wrap - Allows long words to be broken and wrapped to the next line.
word-break - Sets how words should break when reaching the end of a line.
keywords.example: word-break: normal; word-break: break-all; word-break: keep-all;

keywords.description: background - Shorthand property for all background properties.
keywords.example: background: url('image.jpg') no-repeat center/cover; background: linear-gradient(45deg, red, blue);

keywords.description: background-image - Sets background images for an element.
keywords.example: background-image: url('bg.jpg'); background-image: linear-gradient(to right, red, blue);

keywords.description: background-repeat - Sets how background images are repeated.
keywords.example: background-repeat: no-repeat; background-repeat: repeat-x; background-repeat: repeat-y;

keywords.description: background-position - Sets the starting position of background images.
keywords.example: background-position: center; background-position: top right; background-position: 50% 25%;

keywords.description: background-size - Sets the size of background images.
background-attachment - Sets whether background images are fixed or scroll with the page.
keywords.example: background-attachment: scroll; background-attachment: fixed; background-attachment: local;

keywords.description: background-origin - Sets the origin of the background positioning area.
keywords.example: background-origin: border-box; background-origin: padding-box; background-origin: content-box;

keywords.description: background-clip - Sets the painting area of the background.
keywords.example: background-clip: border-box; background-clip: padding-box; background-clip: content-box;

keywords.description: border-width - Sets the width of borders. Can be set for all sides or individually.
border-style - Sets the style of borders (solid, dashed, dotted, etc.).
keywords.example: border-style: solid; border-style: dashed; border-style: dotted; border-style: double;

keywords.description: border-color - Sets the color of borders. Can be set for all sides or individually.
border-radius - Sets rounded corners on elements. Can create circles and complex shapes.
border-top - Shorthand for top border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-top: 2px solid red; border-top: 1px dashed blue;

keywords.description: border-right - Shorthand for right border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-right: 2px solid green; border-right: 1px dotted yellow;

keywords.description: border-bottom - Shorthand for bottom border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-bottom: 2px solid purple; border-bottom: 1px groove orange;

keywords.description: border-left - Shorthand for left border width, style, and color.
keywords.example: border-left: 2px solid pink; border-left: 1px ridge cyan;

keywords.description: outline - Sets outline around elements (outside borders). Used for focus indicators.
keywords.example: outline: 2px solid blue; outline: none; outline: 1px dotted red;

keywords.description: outline-offset - Sets space between outline and border/edge of element.
box-shadow - Adds shadow effects around elements. Can create depth and layering.
text-overflow - Sets how overflowed content that is not displayed should be signaled.
vertical-align - Sets vertical alignment of inline or table-cell elements.
keywords.example: vertical-align: top; vertical-align: middle; vertical-align: baseline;

keywords.description: list-style - Shorthand for list-style-type, list-style-position, and list-style-image.
keywords.example: list-style: none; list-style: disc inside; list-style: square outside;

keywords.description: list-style-type - Sets the type of list-item marker.
keywords.example: list-style-type: disc; list-style-type: decimal; list-style-type: none;

keywords.description: list-style-position - Sets the position of list-item markers.
list-style-image - Sets an image as list-item marker.
cursor - Sets the type of cursor to display when hovering over an element.
keywords.example: cursor: pointer; cursor: grab; cursor: not-allowed; cursor: text;

keywords.description: pointer-events - Controls whether an element can be the target of pointer events.
keywords.example: pointer-events: auto; pointer-events: none; pointer-events: all;

keywords.description: user-select - Controls whether text can be selected by the user.
keywords.example: user-select: none; user-select: auto; user-select: text; user-select: all;

keywords.description: resize - Sets whether an element is resizable by the user.
keywords.example: resize: none; resize: both; resize: horizontal; resize: vertical;

keywords.description: min-width - Sets the minimum width of an element.
max-width - Sets the maximum width of an element.
min-height - Sets the minimum height of an element.
max-height - Sets the maximum height of an element.
box-sizing - Sets how the total width and height of an element is calculated.
margin-top - Sets the top margin of an element.
margin-right - Sets the right margin of an element.
margin-bottom - Sets the bottom margin of an element.
margin-left - Sets the left margin of an element.
padding-top - Sets the top padding of an element.
padding-right - Sets the right padding of an element.
padding-bottom - Sets the bottom padding of an element.
padding-left - Sets the left padding of an element.
flex-direction - Sets the direction of flex items in a flex container.
keywords.example: flex-direction: row; flex-direction: column; flex-direction: row-reverse;

keywords.description: flex-wrap - Sets whether flex items wrap to new lines.
keywords.example: flex-wrap: nowrap; flex-wrap: wrap; flex-wrap: wrap-reverse;

keywords.description: flex-flow - Shorthand for flex-direction and flex-wrap.
keywords.example: flex-flow: row wrap; flex-flow: column nowrap;

keywords.description: justify-content - Aligns flex items along the main axis.
keywords.example: justify-content: center; justify-content: space-between; justify-content: flex-start;

keywords.description: align-items - Aligns flex items along the cross axis.
keywords.example: align-items: center; align-items: stretch; align-items: flex-end;

keywords.description: align-content - Aligns wrapped flex lines.
keywords.example: align-content: center; align-content: space-around; align-content: stretch;

keywords.description: align-self - Overrides align-items for individual flex items.
keywords.example: align-self: auto; align-self: center; align-self: flex-end;

keywords.description: flex-grow - Sets how much a flex item should grow relative to others.
flex-shrink - Sets how much a flex item should shrink relative to others.
flex-basis - Sets the initial main size of a flex item before free space is distributed.
order - Sets the order of flex or grid items without changing HTML structure.
gap - Shorthand for row-gap and column-gap in grid and flexbox layouts.
row-gap - Sets the gap between rows in grid and flexbox layouts.
column-gap - Sets the gap between columns in grid and flexbox layouts.
grid-template-columns - Defines the size and names of grid columns.
grid-template-rows - Defines the size and names of grid rows.
grid-template-areas - Defines grid areas by referencing grid area names.
grid-area - Shorthand for grid-row-start, grid-column-start, grid-row-end, grid-column-end.
grid-column - Shorthand for grid-column-start and grid-column-end.
grid-row - Shorthand for grid-row-start and grid-row-end.
grid-auto-columns - Sets the size of implicitly created grid columns.
grid-auto-rows - Sets the size of implicitly created grid rows.
grid-auto-flow - Controls how auto-placed items get inserted in the grid.
keywords.example: grid-auto-flow: row; grid-auto-flow: column; grid-auto-flow: dense;

keywords.description: place-items - Shorthand for align-items and justify-items in grid layouts.
place-content - Shorthand for align-content and justify-content in grid layouts.
place-self - Shorthand for align-self and justify-self in grid layouts.
filter - Applies graphical effects like blur, brightness, contrast to elements.
keywords.example: filter: blur(5px); filter: brightness(150%); filter: drop-shadow(2px 2px 4px black);

keywords.description: backdrop-filter - Applies graphical effects to the area behind an element.
will-change - Hints to browsers about what properties will change to optimize performance.
keywords.example: will-change: transform; will-change: opacity; will-change: auto;

keywords.description: content - Used with ::before and ::after pseudo-elements to insert content.
counter-increment - Increments one or more CSS counters.
counter-reset - Creates or resets one or more CSS counters.
quotes - Sets quotation marks for different levels of nested quotations.
object-fit - Sets how replaced elements (img, video) should be resized to fit their container.
keywords.example: object-fit: cover; object-fit: contain; object-fit: fill;

keywords.description: object-position - Sets the position of replaced elements within their container.
keywords.example: object-position: center; object-position: top right; object-position: 25% 75%;

keywords.description: aspect-ratio - Sets a preferred aspect ratio for the box.
